event: The Pardon of Saint John Chrysostom
symbols: chains on the kneeling figure — bondage of sin or political captivity awaiting pardon
red episcopal vestments — authority and blood of martyrdom in the Church
woman presenting child — intercession and appeal for mercy
columns — the structure of ecclesiastical authority and the Church
description: A dramatic scene set within a columned interior. At center, a bearded figure in rich red and gold episcopal vestments extends his hand in a gesture of authority or blessing. At lower left, a nearly naked elderly man kneels in chains, his body emaciated, gazing upward with an expression of supplication. At right, a woman in a white head covering holds a small child and looks toward the central figure, while a man in boots kneels beside them. The lighting creates strong chiaroscuro, with the central bishop illuminated against dark architectural shadows.

_meta.title: The Pardon of Saint John Chysostom
